title: carex brownii subsp. transversa
intro: Carex brownii subsp. transversa, also known as the mountain sedge, is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae, native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex brownii subsp. transversa are attributed to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its expectorant properties, which can aid in relieving respiratory congestion. Herbal preparations of Carex brownii subsp. transversa can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant for use in topical creams and ointments.
